# Vote rejection due to diverged logs. Two voters, no witness.
#
# v1 and v2 start with diverged logs: v1 has an extra entry from term b,
# while v2 has entries from a later term c. v2's log is more up-to-date
# (higher term), so v1 cannot win an election.

setup
v1: [a1 b2]
v2: [a1 c2]
----
v1: [a1 b2] term=b
v2: [a1 c2] term=c

# v1 campaigns. v2 rejects: its log term (c) beats v1's (b).
campaign v1
----
v1 campaigns at term c
v1: yes (self)
v2:
  - rejected vote for v1: log not up to date (c2 > b2)
  no, log not up to date (c2 > b2)
outcome: lost

# v2 campaigns. v1 grants: v2's log term (c) is ahead.
campaign v2
----
v2 campaigns at term d
v2: yes (self)
v1:
  - term advance c -> d
  - granted vote for v2 at term d
  yes (term c->d, voted v2)
outcome: won

# v2 replicates to v1. v1's divergent entry b2 is replaced by c2, plus the
# new entries from v2's leadership term.
append v2
----
v2 appends through d3
v1:
  - accepted append through d3 (ok, replaced 1 entries)
  ok, replaced 1 entries (log=[a1 | c2 d3])
v1: [a1 c2 d3] term=d
v2: [a1 c2 d3] term=d committed=d3 leader
